title: NASA programs feel effects of workforce reductions
summary: NASA projects are impacted by a 20% reduction in its civil servant workforce over the past year. Over two dozen major programs have reported effects from these workforce reductions.

title: Poland commits $745 million to EU’s IRIS² constellation project
summary: Poland has committed $745 million to the EU's IRIS² satellite internet constellation project. This investment will support the development of a multi-orbit satellite internet system.

title: Mark Matossian co-founds Whipsmart Ventures to fund space and deep tech
summary: Mark Matossian has co-founded Whipsmart Ventures, a venture capital fund aimed at investing in space and deep tech startups. The fund is established in collaboration with a software engineer and a best-selling author.

title: Will Bruey on rethinking the role of pharmaceuticals in low Earth orbit
summary: Varda CEO Will Bruey discusses the company's role in bridging pharmaceutical development with low Earth orbit at the Ascend conference. The conversation highlights how Varda aims to leverage space for medical advancements.

title: Airbus space business takes advantage of growing demand for satellite systems
summary: Airbus's space business has rebounded due to increasing demand for satellite systems, particularly in Europe. The company is leveraging this growth to pursue a joint venture.

title: China’s Shijian-31 satellite is sweeping GEO using a unique orbit
summary: China's Shijian-31 satellite, launched in June, is operating in a unique orbit that provides a distinctive view of the geostationary belt. This classified satellite's maneuvering suggests advanced capabilities in orbital operations.

title: FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G
summary: The FCC approved significant space industry reforms on July 22, including clearing more C-band spectrum for a 5G auction next year. The changes aim to accelerate the commercial space economy through a new licensing overhaul.

title: Vast establishes division for national security projects
summary: Vast has created a new division focused on national security space projects. This move highlights the company's expansion into defense-related space initiatives.

title: The strategic competition with China: winning the multi-domain race
summary: The article discusses the strategic competition with China, emphasizing the need to win the multi-domain race in military innovation. It highlights how historical military advancements have reshaped warfare and rendered past strategies obsolete.

title: Relativity Space to expand Terran R production in Florida
summary: Relativity Space has partnered with Space Florida to establish manufacturing and testing facilities for its Terran R rocket on Florida's Space Coast. This expansion aims to boost production capacity for the company's next-generation launch vehicle.

title: Vulcan Centaur swapped out again: NASA goes with SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket for launch of space weather mission
summary: NASA's SunRISE mission will now launch on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy instead of a ULA Vulcan Centaur. The change reflects ongoing adjustments in launch vehicle assignments.

title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4: Release date & how to watch Paramount+’s sensational sci-fi series
summary: Paramount+ announced the release date for 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4. The sci-fi series is highly anticipated by fans.

title: Scientists may have finally solved the mystery of the sun's missing silver
summary: Scientists have revised their understanding of the sun's composition, finding it contains slightly more silver than previously believed. This discovery helps resolve a long-standing mystery about the sun's elemental makeup.

title: How much better is the Canon EOS R6 III than previous models for astrophotography?
summary: The Canon EOS R6 III offers an 8 MP resolution increase, a CFexpress card slot, and improved low-light autofocusing compared to the R6 II, but its benefits for astrophotography are uncertain.

title: NASA's retired astronauts are hitting the road to get young people to vote: 'one small step for you, and one giant leap for our country.'
summary: NASA's retired astronauts are encouraging young people to vote, framing it as a civic duty. They plan to visit colleges and universities to spread this message.

title: Satellite spots a 'hummingbird' in Antarctica | Space photo of the day for July 23, 2026
summary: A satellite image from NISAR captured a hummingbird-like shape in Antarctica, which was later identified as a mountaintop. The discovery was highlighted in a space photo of the day for July 23, 2026.

title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds'' season 4 opener is a pulpy sci-fi joy packed with dinosaurs, martians, and a 5th planet where the asteroid belt should be
summary: The season 4 premiere of 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' features dinosaurs, Martians, and a fifth planet in the asteroid belt, blending pulpy sci-fi adventure with high-stakes themes for the Enterprise crew.

title: Should Artemis IV astronauts actually land near the moon's south pole? Artemis II pilot says the lunar equator might be better
summary: NASA's Artemis program initially planned to land astronauts near the moon's south pole, but Artemis II pilot Victor Glover suggests targeting the lunar equator for a safer and easier landing. The debate highlights differing opinions on mission priorities and risks.

title: The James Webb Space Telescope's disappearing 'Little Red Dots' may lead to another cosmic puzzle
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope's 'Little Red Dots' may disappear, presenting a new cosmic mystery. This phenomenon could be akin to how dinosaurs evolved into birds.

title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
summary: China's Gravity-1 rocket, the world's most powerful solid rocket, successfully launched nine satellites into orbit from a ship at sea. This marks Orienspace's third mission, setting a new record for the company.

title: Starship Flight 13 moved to Friday due to weather
summary: Starship Flight 13's launch has been postponed to Friday due to weather conditions. SpaceX will attempt the launch again on the new date.

title: Soyuz MS-29 crew arrives at ISS, U.S. EVA completes Canadarm2 repairs
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 crew successfully arrived at the International Space Station (ISS), expanding the station's crew by three members. A U.S. extravehicular activity (EVA) completed repairs on the Canadarm2 robotic arm.

title: Path to Starship Flight 13 requires additional Booster 20 test
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Starship Flight 13, targeting a launch no earlier than July 23, but requires additional testing of Booster 20. The delay highlights ongoing challenges in the Starship program's development.

title: Falcon 9 to launch MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is scheduled to launch the MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman. This mission aims to demonstrate in-space servicing capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a new attempt at the Starship Flight 13 launch after last week's abort. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are scheduled for upcoming launches.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space are making progress on their respective launch vehicles: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5. While Starship's test program has been in the spotlight, these companies continue advancing their rocket developments.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a rocket booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another crewed mission to the orbiting laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are planned globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step toward its upcoming mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The silicon brains steering next-generation antennas
summary: Next-generation antennas are being enhanced with advanced silicon-based technology to improve performance and efficiency. This innovation is expected to revolutionize communication systems in the space industry.

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: ESA's PLATO spacecraft has completed key electromagnetic compatibility tests in the Maxwell Test Chamber, confirming its electronics are fit for space. The mission is now cleared for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in March 2027.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 ministerial meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying operations to create artificial eclipses. This marks a significant step forward for the mission's scientific objectives.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: NASA Puts $2B Price Tag on Nuclear Mars Mission
summary: NASA has estimated a $2 billion cost for the Space Reactor-1 Freedom, a fission-powered spacecraft intended for Mars missions. This would be the first interplanetary spacecraft powered by nuclear fission.

title: Intel Authorization Act Proposes Limiting Commercial EO Access
summary: The Intel Authorization Act seeks to restrict commercial Earth observation (EO) satellite operators from sharing data with the public, framing EO data as a privilege rather than a right. This proposed legislation aims to give the US government greater control over the dissemination of EO satellite imagery.

title: The UK Scraps the Department in Charge of UKSA
summary: The UK has eliminated the department overseeing the UK Space Agency (UKSA) during the Farnborough Air Show. New Prime Minister Andy Burnham took the opportunity to dismantle the agency amid the country's aerospace celebrations.

title: The UK and Space Florida Launch Joint Fund
summary: The UK and Space Florida have launched a joint fund to support projects with partners from both regions. This initiative aims to foster collaboration between the UK and Florida's space sectors.

title: Breaking Down the $686B Space Economy
summary: The global space economy reached a record $686 billion in 2025, as reported by the Space Foundation. This marks significant growth in the industry's economic impact.

title: FCC Eyes Wi-Fi and Bluetooth Bands for Satellites
summary: The FCC is considering a proposal to permit unlicensed wireless devices like Wi-Fi and Bluetooth to communicate directly with satellites. This move aims to expand satellite connectivity options for consumer devices.

title: deltaVision Raises €10.2M to Build In-Orbit Refueling Tech
summary: deltaVision has raised €10.2 million to develop in-orbit refueling technology. The funds will support expanding manufacturing capacity and researching new capabilities.

title: UK Commits £62M into Domestic Space Capabilities
summary: The UK government allocated £62 million ($83.3 million) to boost domestic satellite communications and space technologies. This investment aims to enhance the UK's space capabilities.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace achieved a milestone by becoming the first private Indian company to reach orbit with its Vikram-1 rocket, launched from India's Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its national security space launch contract ceiling by $11.4 billion, tripling the pool from $5.6B to $17B. This expansion reflects growing investment in secure space launch capabilities.

title: Space Is Coming for Silicon
summary: SpaceX has introduced Starmind, an AI satellite megaconstellation, marking a significant step in orbital data centers. Elon Musk highlighted the project as part of SpaceX's expanding space-based infrastructure.

title: Iceye and KT SAT Partner on Flood Intelligence in South Korea
summary: Iceye and KT SAT have partnered to provide flood monitoring solutions in South Korea for government and commercial organizations. This is the first deployment of Iceye's solutions in the country.

title: Intellian Releases New Terminal for Iridium GMDSS Service
summary: Intellian has launched a new terminal for Iridium's GMDSS maritime safety service, utilizing the Iridium Certus 100 service. The terminal was announced to enhance maritime distress and safety communications.

title: Frequency Electronics Reports Increased Demand From Proliferated Satellite Programs
summary: Frequency Electronics reports $18 million in recent contract wins, including an $11 million award from an undisclosed major aerospace prime contractor to support a proliferated satellite program.

title: FCC Approves a New C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC approved a new C-band auction to clear 160 megahertz of the upper C-band spectrum, building on previous efforts. The auction aims to further allocate spectrum for wireless services.

title: Lite Coms Wins $22M Contract to Support Operational Readiness
summary: Lite Coms secured a $22 million contract from an unnamed government and defense customer to provide satellite communications for operational readiness. The company specializes in integration and manufacturing of satellite communication solutions.

title: FCC Overhauls Satellite Licensing, Adopts ‘Assembly Line’ Approach
summary: The FCC approved a major overhaul of its satellite and earth station licensing process, adopting a streamlined 'licensing assembly line' approach. This change aims to modernize and expedite the approval process for space-related applications.

title: Iridium Reports 4% Revenue Growth in Q2 Amid Rocket Lab Acquisition Deal
summary: Iridium reported a 4% revenue growth in Q2 while undergoing an acquisition deal with Rocket Lab. The satellite operator's financial performance and acquisition status were highlighted in their latest report.

title: Contrivian Completes Merger With Big Network to Fuel Orchestration Service
summary: Contrivian has finalized its merger with Big Network to create a vertically integrated connectivity platform. The deal aims to enhance multi-modal orchestration services in the space industry.

title: SpaceX Launches Northrop Grumman’s First Mission Robotic Vehicle
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Northrop Grumman's first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV-MEP) for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The mission marks a significant step in satellite servicing and refueling technology.
